a.notes: get has three def sites (two @overload stubs at lines 123-127 plus implementation at 129) collapsing to one set element. 'name: Any' at line 99 is an annotation-only declaration with no value, hence not a binding assignment and excluded.

b.notes: LookupDict directly contains one class-body assignment and its methods; repeated overload definitions of get normalize to one set element.

a.notes: 19 direct defs plus the environment_class assignment (line 1152). Annotation-only declarations at lines 1154-1162 (environment, globals, name, filename, blocks, root_render_func, _module, _debug_info, _uptodate) have no value and bind no name, so they are excluded. Environment.template_class = Template at line 1666 is an attribute assignment outside the class body, not a child.

Body (l367) has one call expression: self.__class__(self.obj[key], self.escape). self.__class__ resolves to _MarkupEscapeHelper (no subclasses exist in the repo), i.e. re-instantiation of the helper. self.obj[key] is a subscript, not a call; self.escape is passed, not called.

b.notes: The call through self.__class__ is dynamically dispatched and its runtime callee cannot be fully determined.

a.notes: l235 escape(s): the bare name resolves lexically to the module-level escape function (class-body names are not visible from method bodies). l238 cls(rv): cls is Markup for direct use and no Markup subclasses exist in the repo, so resolved to instantiation of Markup.

b.notes: escape resolves statically, but the conditional call cls(rv) invokes the runtime class passed as cls and cannot be fully determined.

a.notes: self.get_signature resolves lexically to the method defined on SigningAlgorithm (line 20); subclass overrides (NoneAlgorithm/HMACAlgorithm/_ReverseAlgorithm) may receive the dispatch at runtime.

b.notes: The self.get_signature call uses dynamic method dispatch, so its runtime callee cannot be determined fully.

a.notes: Calls: TypeVar('T') from typing (external) and has(A)/has(A[str]) x2. has is imported from attr, and src/attr/__init__.py imports has from ._funcs, so the called def is src/attr/_funcs.py::has. class A(Generic[T]) and A[str] subscription are not call expressions.

b.notes: The three has(...) calls refer to a repository-imported callable, but its definition is not shown, so its exact internal symbol cannot be determined.

a.notes: wrapt.partial (line 2410) cannot be resolved to a def: src/wrapt/__init__.py source is not shown and the attribute may be a re-export; wrapper() (line 2413) calls the runtime value returned by it. Object(...) at line 2400 instantiates the locally nested class.

b.notes: Calls through self.assertRaises, self.assertNotEqual, and wrapper cannot be resolved statically; the definition site of wrapt.partial is not shown.

a.notes: tz.datetime_ambiguous (line 2586) is repo-internal (from dateutil import tz) but the dateutil.tz source is not shown, so its defining file/qualpath cannot be determined; that call site blocks a full answer. self._get_ambig_error_tzinfo resolves to the method defined at line 2552 in the same class.

TzFoldMixin is a mix-in: self.gettz (a NotImplementedError stub here), self._get_tzname and self._gettz_context are overridden in subclasses (TzLocalNixTest, GettzTest, ZoneInfoGettzTest, TZRangeTest, TZStrTest, TZICalTest), so runtime callees depend on dynamic dispatch; the lexical mixin defs are listed. tz.UTC is an attribute access, not a call.

b.notes: Runtime dispatch for self._gettz_context and self.gettz depends on the concrete subclass, so their callees cannot be fully determined statically.

a.notes: Calls in body: @staticmethod decorator on nested def sm (counts as a call), varnames(...) twice (imported from pluggy._hooks -> repo module src/pluggy/_hooks.py), and MyClass() instantiation of the nested class.

Direct children of class CharInfo: the __slots__ assignment (line 51) and def __init__ (line 99). Lines 76-97 are annotation-only statements (name: type with no value); they are not assignments and bind nothing, so excluded.

b.notes: Complete direct class-body definitions and simple-name assignments are shown.

a.notes: Calls: len(data), sorted(data), int(...) twice, ceil(p) (from math import), and p.is_integer() where p = n*percentile/100 is a float (true division), best-effort ext:float.is_integer. No repo-internal callees.

b.notes: The p.is_integer call uses dynamic attribute dispatch, so its runtime callee cannot be fully determined from the shown source.

a.notes: Body is a single call s.encode("punycode"); s is annotated str, so the callee is the built-in str.encode method. No repo-internal callees.

All method receivers are locally typed: s is str when s.split is called, result = [] is a list (result.append), _unicode_dots_re is a module-level re.compile pattern (re.Pattern.split), b"." is bytes (bytes.join). str(s, "ascii") is the builtin str constructor; IDNAError instantiation is a repo-internal class call.
Body (line 264) is sentinel.append(sender); sentinel is bound to a list literal in the enclosing function (line 261), so the callee is list.append. No repo-internal callees.

All imports top-level. 'from tenacity import RetryCallState, retry': RetryCallState is a symbol of package tenacity -> repo:tenacity/__init__.py; 'retry' names the module file tenacity/retry.py in the package -> repo:tenacity/retry.py plus ancestor __init__. 'import asyncio' does not resolve to repo tenacity/asyncio (no top-level asyncio package at any resolution root) -> external.
Body (lines 1855-1880) contains only two call expressions: ', '.join(...) and ValueError(...). Membership tests, slicing and f-strings are not calls; no repo-internal callees.
